schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme "Imbichi Bawa Housing Renovation Scheme" by the Minority Welfare Department, Government of Kerala, aims to improve the basic facilities of houses belonging to widowed, divorced, or abandoned women from minority religious communities. Through this scheme, financial assistance is provided for house repairs to eligible women belonging to Muslim, Christian, Buddhist, Sikh, Parsi, and Jain communities. The scheme is implemented by the District Collectorates under the Minority Welfare Department.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - A non-refundable grant of ₹50,000/- is provided for the repair of a house. - This financial assistance is intended for improving basic amenities in houses that lack proper windows, doors, roofing, flooring, finishing, plumbing, sanitation, or electrification.
schemeContent.exclusions_md: - The applicant should not have children with a permanent income from government or semi-government institutions. - The applicant should not have received financial assistance for house construction from the government or similar agencies within the last 10 years.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme "Imbichi Bawa Housing Renovation Scheme" by the Minority Welfare Department, Government of Kerala, aims to improve the basic facilities of houses belonging to widowed, divorced, or abandoned women from minority religious communities.

children.text: The scheme "Imbichi Bawa Housing Renovation Scheme" by the Minority Welfare Department, Government of Kerala, aims to improve the basic facilities of houses belonging to widowed, divorced, or abandoned women from minority religious communities. Through this scheme, financial assistance is provided for house repairs to eligible women belonging to Muslim, Christian, Buddhist, Sikh, Parsi, and Jain communities. The scheme is implemented by the District Collectorates under the Minority Welfare Department.
A non-refundable grant of ₹50,000/- is provided for the repair of a house.
This financial assistance is intended for improving basic amenities in houses that lack proper windows, doors, roofing, flooring, finishing, plumbing, sanitation, or electrification.
The applicant should not have children with a permanent income from government or semi-government institutions.
The applicant should not have received financial assistance for house construction from the government or similar agencies within the last 10 years.
Step 1:
Visit to
official website

of the Directorate of Minority Affairs. In the "News & Updates" section, click on "Application invited for Imbichi Bawa Housing Renovation scheme 2025-26" and download the application form. Take a printout of the application form.
Step 2:

In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 3:
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to one of the following address -
Minority Welfare Section of the respective District Collectorate.
(By Post) Deputy Collector (General), District Minority Welfare Section, District Collectorate.

applicationProcess.process_md: **Step 1:****** Visit to [official website](https://minoritywelfare.kerala.gov.in/) of the Directorate of Minority Affairs. In the "News & Updates" section, click on "Application invited for Imbichi Bawa Housing Renovation scheme 2025-26" and download the application form. Take a printout of the application form. **Step 2:****** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required). **Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to one of the following address - - Minority Welfare Section of the respective District Collectorate. - (By Post) Deputy Collector (General), District Minority Welfare Section, District Collectorate.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: - The applicant must belong to one of the following minority religious communities: Muslim, Christian, Buddhist, Sikh, Parsi, or Jain. - The applicant must be a widow, divorced woman, or abandoned woman. - The house, owned by the applicant or her spouse, should not exceed 1200 square feet in area. - The applicant must be the sole breadwinner of the family. - The house should lack basic facilities such as proper windows, doors, roofing, flooring, finishing, plumbing, sanitation, or electrification. > **Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation** _*Preference is given to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families._ _*Preference is given to applicants whose children face physical or mental challenges._ _*Preference is given to applicants who have only daughters._ _*Preference is given to applicants who do not have children._

children.text: The applicant must belong to one of the following minority religious communities: Muslim, Christian, Buddhist, Sikh, Parsi, or Jain.
The applicant must be a widow, divorced woman, or abandoned woman.
The house, owned by the applicant or her spouse, should not exceed 1200 square feet in area.
The applicant must be the sole breadwinner of the family.
The house should lack basic facilities such as proper windows, doors, roofing, flooring, finishing, plumbing, sanitation, or electrification.
Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation
*Preference is given to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families.
*Preference is given to applicants whose children face physical or mental challenges.
*Preference is given to applicants who have only daughters.
*Preference is given to applicants who do not have children.
